---
title: Imports
jupyter: python3
---



```{python}
import os

import scanpy as sc
import anndata as ad
import scvi
```

# Subset GongSharma data

```{python}
folder_path = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "Sikkema_data")
files_in_folder = os.listdir(folder_path)
files_in_folder
```

```{python}
adata_path = os.path.join(folder_path, files_in_folder[0])
adata = sc.read_h5ad(adata_path, backed='r')
```

```{python}
adata
```

```{python}
print(adata.X[:5, :5].toarray())
# scVI noramlized values are in X but we want the raw counts
```

```{python}
if adata.raw is not None:
    print("Raw counts found in adata.raw")
    # To access the first 5x5 of raw data:
    print(adata.raw.X[:5, :5].toarray()) 
else:
    print("No data in adata.raw")
```

```{python}
sc.pl.umap(adata, color="ann_level_1")
```

```{python}
len(adata.obs.donor_id.unique())
```

```{python}
adata.obs['Sample'] = adata.obs['donor_id']

var_names = adata.var_names

# Create clean adata object
adata = ad.AnnData(
    X = adata.raw[:, adata.var_names].X,
    obs = adata.obs
)
adata.var_names = var_names
```

```{python}
print("Preview of new X (should be counts):")
print(adata.X[:5, :5].toarray())
```

```{python}
output_file_name = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "data", "Sikkema")

adata.write_h5ad(output_file_name + '.h5ad', compression="gzip")
adata.obs.to_feather(output_file_name + '.feather')
```

```{python}
adata
```

```{python}
adata.var_names[:5].tolist()
```

```{python}
sample_column = 'sample' 

# Use value_counts() to get the count for each sample
cell_counts = adata.obs[sample_column].value_counts().reset_index()
cell_counts.columns = [sample_column, 'cell_count']

print("Cell Counts per Sample:")
print(cell_counts)
```

```{python}
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Check if the cell_counts DataFrame was successfully created
if 'cell_count' in cell_counts.columns:
    # Set plot style
    sns.set_style("whitegrid")
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))

    # Create the bar plot
    sns.barplot(
        x=sample_column,
        y='cell_count',
        data=cell_counts,
        palette='viridis' # Choose a nice color palette
    )

    # Customize plot labels and title
    plt.title('Number of Cells per Sample', fontsize=16)
    plt.xlabel('Sample ID', fontsize=12)
    plt.ylabel('Number of Cells', fontsize=12)
    
    # Rotate x-axis labels if sample names are long
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha='right') 
    
    # Add the count numbers on top of the bars
    for index, row in cell_counts.iterrows():
        plt.text(
            index, 
            row['cell_count'] + max(cell_counts['cell_count']) * 0.01, # Position slightly above the bar
            f"{row['cell_count']:,}", # Format number with comma
            color='black', 
            ha='center', 
            fontsize=10
        )

    plt.tight_layout() # Adjust layout to prevent labels from being cut off
    plt.show()
```
